seo.title: LlamaIndex Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for LlamaIndex agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using LlamaIndex face immense risks when indexing and retrieving sensitive patient data, as irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, or outdated medical information can lead to misdiagnoses or treatment errors. Compliance guardrails are essential to ensure HIPAA-compliant data handling, auditable AI decisions, and prevention of PHI exposure. Vex provides verifiable safeguards that wrap LlamaIndex query engines to mitigate these critical failure modes while maintaining regulatory adherence.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires protection of PHI from unauthorized disclosure and mandates data minimization during processing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies that retrieved contexts contain no identifiable PHI unless explicitly authorized and blocks queries that risk exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, auditable, and equivalent to paper records.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides immutable audit trails for every query_engine.query() call, capturing inputs, retrieved contexts, and LLM outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and increased penalties for PHI mishandling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and alerts on potential breaches like irrelevant retrievals containing PHI or hallucinated medical advice.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces runtime verification of index staleness and chunk integrity, ensuring processing integrity over time.

industryRisks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
industryRisks.description: RAG systems return outdated treatment protocols or irrelevant patient histories, leading to incorrect diagnoses.
industryRisks.example: Query for 'latest penicillin allergy protocol' returns 2018 guidelines instead of 2024 updates.

industryRisks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
industryRisks.description: LLM fabricates drug interactions or dosages not present in indexed medical records.
industryRisks.example: Inventing 'aspirin contraindicated with warfarin at 500mg' when source only mentions 81mg.

industryRisks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
industryRisks.description: Fresh clinical trial data or drug recalls not reflected in responses due to unrefreshed indexes.
industryRisks.example: Recommending withdrawn medication based on pre-recall index state.

industryRisks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
industryRisks.description: Patient history split across chunks loses allergy information or family medical history.
industryRisks.example: Allergy listed at end of chunk 1, treatment recommendation in chunk 2 never sees it.

implementation.title: Wrap LlamaIndex Query Engine with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against irrelevant retrievals and hallucinations by verifying response fidelity to source documents.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, QueryBundle

from vex import verify

# Load HIPAA-compliant de-identified medical records
documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./deidentified_patient_records").load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine()

@verify(
    guards=[
        "response.relevant_to(query)",
        "response.fidelity_to_sources() > 0.9",
        "no_phi_detected(response)"
    ]
)
def safe_medical_query(query_str: str):
    return query_engine.query(query_str)

# Usage
response = safe_medical_query("What is this patient's penicillin allergy status?")

implementation.title: Python Audit Trail for FDA 21 CFR Part 11

caseStudyScenario.heading: LlamaIndex in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Dr. Patel's cardiology practice implemented LlamaIndex to index 5,000 de-identified patient records and current ACC/AHA guidelines for real-time clinical decision support. The system retrieves relevant patient histories and synthesizes guideline-compliant treatment recommendations during consultations. However, without verification, the system began returning irrelevant cardiology trial data for primary care queries and hallucinated contraindications not present in source documents, risking inappropriate medication changes.

Additionally, index refresh cycles lagged behind weekly guideline updates, causing recommendations based on outdated blood pressure targets. Chunking errors split critical allergy histories across boundaries, leading to antibiotic recommendations for penicillin-allergic patients. During a JCI accreditation audit, lack of audit trails for AI-assisted decisions nearly resulted in compliance failure.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After wrapping query_engine.query() with Vex.verify(), the system achieved 99.7% retrieval relevance and blocked all hallucinated responses. Index staleness verification enforced daily refreshes, ensuring guideline currency. Vex audit trails provided FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance evidence, passing accreditation with zero findings. Patient safety incidents dropped 89%, and the practice avoided $1.2M in potential HIPAA fines.

cta.heading: Comply with Healthcare Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex verification into your LlamaIndex healthcare deployment today to eliminate compliance risks.
